handlers.h(3) Library Functions Manual handlers.h(3)

NAME

handlers.h - ACIP protocol packet handlers (transport-agnostic).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acip_client_callbacks_t
Client-side packet handler callbacks. struct acip_server_callbacks_t
Server-side packet handler callbacks.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acip_handle_client_packet (acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t type, const void *payload, size_t payload_len, const acip_client_callbacks_t *callbacks)
Handle incoming packet on client side. asciichat_error_t acip_handle_server_packet (acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t type, const void *payload, size_t payload_len, void *client_ctx, const acip_server_callbacks_t *callbacks)
Handle incoming packet on server side.

Detailed Description

ACIP protocol packet handlers (transport-agnostic).

Provides handler functions for all ACIP packet types (6000-6199). Handlers are transport-agnostic - they work with any acip_transport_t.

DESIGN PATTERN:

Handlers use callback pattern to decouple protocol logic from application logic. Applications register callbacks for events they care about.

Example usage:

// Define application callbacks
void my_on_ascii_frame(const ascii_frame_packet_t *frame, const void *data, size_t len, void *ctx) {


  // Render frame to terminal
}
// Register callbacks
acip_client_callbacks_t callbacks = {


  .on_ascii_frame = my_on_ascii_frame,


  .app_ctx = my_app_state
};
// Process incoming packet
acip_handle_client_packet(transport, type, payload, payload_len, &callbacks);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file handlers.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t acip_handle_client_packet (acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t type, const void * payload, size_t payload_len, const acip_client_callbacks_t * callbacks)

Handle incoming packet on client side. Dispatches packet to appropriate callback based on type. Transport-agnostic - works with TCP, WebSocket, etc.

Parameters

transport Transport instance
type Packet type
payload Packet payload
payload_len Payload length
callbacks Application callbacks

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Definition at line 196 of file network/acip/handlers.c.

197                                                                                                           {
198   if (!transport || !callbacks) {
199     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or callbacks");
200   }
201   (void)transport;
202 
203   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
204   int idx = handler_hash_lookup(g_client_handler_hash, type);
205   if (idx < 0) {
206     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unhandled client packet type: %d", type);
207   }
208 
209   return g_client_handlers[idx](payload, payload_len, callbacks);
210 }




References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acip_client_receive_and_dispatch().






asciichat_error_t acip_handle_server_packet
  (acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t type, const void *
  payload, size_t payload_len, void * client_ctx, const
  acip_server_callbacks_t * callbacks)


Handle incoming packet on server side. Dispatches packet to
    appropriate callback based on type. Transport-agnostic - works with TCP,
    WebSocket, etc.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


type Packet type


payload Packet payload


payload_len Payload length


client_ctx Per-client context (e.g., client_info_t*)


callbacks Application callbacks


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 687 of file
    network/acip/handlers.c.


689                                                                                       {
690   if (!transport || !callbacks) {
691     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or callbacks");
692   }
693   (void)transport;
694 
695   // DEBUG: Log all packet types received
696   log_info("ACIP_HANDLE: Received packet type=%d (0x%04x), payload_len=%zu", type, type, payload_len);
697 
698   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
699   int idx = handler_hash_lookup(g_server_handler_hash, type);
700   if (idx < 0) {
701     log_error("ACIP_HANDLER_NOT_FOUND: No handler for packet type=%d (0x%04x, name=%s)", type, type,
702               g_packet_type_name(type));
703     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unhandled server packet type: %d", type);
704   }
705 
706   log_info("📥 [HANDLER_DISPATCH] Calling handler: type=%d (%s), handler_idx=%d, payload=%zu bytes, client_ctx=%p",
707            type, g_packet_type_name(type), idx, payload_len, client_ctx);
708   asciichat_error_t result = g_server_handlers[idx](payload, payload_len, client_ctx, callbacks);
709 
710   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
711     log_error("❌ [HANDLER_ERROR] Handler failed: type=%d (%s), result=%d", type, g_packet_type_name(type), result);
712   } else {
713     log_info("✅ [HANDLER_COMPLETE] Handler succeeded: type=%d (%s), payload=%zu bytes processed", type,
714              g_packet_type_name(type), payload_len);
715   }
716 
717   return result;
718 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_error, log_info, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(), and
    client_dispatch_thread().
